title: How to Do NZ on a Family Budget — Real Costs and Where to Cut
meta_desc: Explore NZ with your family on a budget, discover real costs and money-saving tips
intro: A family trip around New Zealand can be expensive, but with some planning, you can have a fantastic time without breaking the bank. For a family of four, a daily budget of $250-350 NZD can cover accommodation, food, and activities. In this guide, we'll show you how to make the most of your NZ family travel budget.

sections.heading: Understanding Your NZ Family Travel Budget
sections.body: When planning your trip, it's essential to consider all the costs involved, including accommodation, food, transportation, and activities. A realistic weekly spend for a family of four can range from $1,750 to $2,450 NZD, depending on your choices of accommodation and activities.

sections.heading: Free Activities in NZ
list: Visit the Auckland War Memorial Museum (free admission on the last Tuesday of every month)
Explore the scenic coastal walk in Wellington (free)
Go hiking in the beautiful Queenstown gardens (free)

sections.heading: Transportation Costs in NZ
sections.body: If you're planning to drive around NZ, you can expect to pay around $80-120 NZD per day for a campervan rental, depending on the season and the type of vehicle. Alternatively, you can use public transportation, which can cost around $20-50 NZD per person for a one-way trip, depending on the route and the mode of transport. For example, a drive from Auckland to Rotorua takes around 3 hours, while a bus ride takes around 4 hours and costs around $30-50 NZD per person.

sections.heading: Realistic Daily Expenses for a Family of Four
Getting the Most Out of Your NZ Family Travel Budget
sections.body: To make the most of your budget, consider visiting during the off-season (April to May or September to November), when prices are lower. You can also save money by cooking your own meals instead of eating out, and by choosing free or low-cost activities, such as hiking or visiting local markets.
